strength.title: Intensification Strength
strength.stimulus: Increase intensity with heavier loads and fewer reps. Volume stays moderate, intensity climbs.

structure.heading: Warm-up (10 min)
items: Dynamic: leg swings, hip circles, thoracic rotations
2 sets: bodyweight squats x 10, push-ups x 8
2-3 lighter sets building to working weight

structure.heading: Back Squat (20 min)
items: Heavier than last week, but still 2 reps in reserve

structure.heading: Bench Press (15 min)
items: Pair with weighted pull-ups or bodyweight pull-ups x max reps

structure.heading: Accessory Circuit (15 min)
items: Dumbbell row 10 each side (heavier than last week)

structure.heading: Warm-up (5 min)
items: 10 squats, 10 push-ups, 5 sets of progressive loading

notes.about: Week 2 bumps the intensity. Loads go up, reps come down. RPE 7-8 means the last rep should feel hard — you have 1-2 reps left in the tank. This is where you start building real strength. The kettlebell swings in the accessory section train your hip hinge pattern — the same pattern you'll use on the sled push.
notes.week_context: Intensification week — heavier loads, slightly fewer reps. You should finish feeling challenged but not crushed. If a set feels easy, add weight next set.
notes.block_context: Second week of the 4-week block. Volume has dropped slightly, intensity has climbed. Next week we push peak performance.

running.title: Aerobic Base + Tempo
running.stimulus: Introduce threshold work. Build aerobic engine with easy volume, add one moderate tempo push to begin lactic threshold work.

structure.heading: Warm-up (10 min)
items: 10 min easy jog, gradually building pace
Dynamic drills last 2 min: A-skips, B-skips, strides x 3

structure.heading: Main Session (45 min)
items: 15 min tempo run (comfortably hard — can speak in short phrases but not hold conversation)

notes.about: Tempo running is the sweet spot between easy and hard. It trains your lactic threshold — the pace at which your body can still clear lactate as fast as it produces it. Improving this makes race pace feel sustainable. Don't confuse tempo with all-out — you should finish the tempo portion feeling you could have pushed another 5 minutes, but not 15.
notes.week_context: Intensification week — first introduction of tempo work. Listen to your body. If you feel run down, extend the easy portion and shorten the tempo.
notes.block_context: Running progression: Week 1 all easy, Week 2 adds tempo, Week 3 race-pace intervals, Week 4 deload.

engine.title: Station Circuit Intro
engine.stimulus: Build work capacity through race-specific stations combined with running. Moderate-high pace, focus on transitions.

structure.heading: Warm-up (10 min)
items: Dynamic: walking lunges, torso twists, arm circles
2 sets: 10 wall balls, 10 air squats, 5 burpees

structure.heading: Cool-down (10 min)
items: 5 min stretching: hip flexors, quads, shoulders

notes.about: Higher pace than last week, more rounds, more station volume. The focus is still transitions — how smoothly you move from run to station and back. Sandbag cleans are new this week — they train your hip hinge with a dynamic load, crucial for the race's sandbag lunges station.
notes.week_context: Intensification week — work rate increases. You should feel this one. Rest between rounds is your friend — use it to catch your breath.
notes.block_context: Engine progression: familiarization → increased pace → race pace → deload.

emom.title: Intensification EMOM
emom.stimulus: Higher rep counts across EMOM rounds to stress work capacity. Pacing discipline still primary focus.

structure.heading: EMOM 28 min
items: Aim for 5-15 seconds of rest per work minute

structure.heading: Accessory Core (12 min)
items: Toes to bar or hanging leg raises

notes.about: Reps per minute go up this week. You're training your ability to sustain work output across a longer session. If a minute feels impossible, scale the reps — consistency across 7 rounds matters more than crushing round 1 and dying at round 4. Pacing is a skill. Build it today.
notes.week_context: Intensification — more reps, more rounds. Scale if needed.
notes.block_context: EMOM progression: establish → increase → peak → deload.

simulation.title: Compromised Running Threshold
simulation.stimulus: Practice running at threshold pace after station work. The core hybrid race challenge.

structure.heading: Warm-up (10 min)
items: Practice: 5 wall balls, 30m sled push at light weight

structure.heading: Main Session (40 min)
items: Focus: maintain pace on 4th round — don't blow up early

notes.about: Today is the most race-specific session of the week. Running at tempo pace with tired legs is exactly what happens in a Hyrox race. Pay attention to how your pace feels — can you maintain round 1's pace on round 4? If you blow up after round 2, you went too hard early. Learn your sustainable pace today.
notes.week_context: Intensification simulation — threshold pace, more volume than last week.
notes.block_context: Simulation progression: moderate → threshold → race-pace → deload tune-up.

notes.about: Week 2 of the block. Intensity climbs across all sessions.
notes.week_context: Intensification: heavier loads, faster paces, more volume. You should feel this week.
notes.block_context: Second of four weeks in this block. Peak week coming next.
